title: Teso Life is opening a 20,000-square-foot Downtown Crossing store Friday
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Local News The popular Asian lifestyle chain is taking over the former Forever 21 space in Downtown Crossing.
Teso Life opens June 26 at 459 Washington St.
in Downtown Boston.

title: Your next Uber or Lyft ride in Boston could be a taxi
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Local News A new pilot program will allow cab drivers to accept ride requests through ride-hailing apps for the first time.
Drivers wait in the taxi pool near Boston Logan International Airport.
(Craig F.

title: Photos: Here are the best scenes from the England-Ghana World Cup matchup at ‘Boston Stadium’
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: World Cup It was another massive crowd in Foxborough, even if the outcome was somewhat underwhelming.
Ghana fans were the life of the party during the World Cup draw against England at "Boston Stadium.
" Via Hussein Hammouda/Boston.

title: City councilor proposes making 3 a.m. last call permanent in Boston
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Local News Boston bars and restaurants can extend their last call until July 31 under a recently-signed bill.
Scotland fans celebrate outside of The White Bull Tavern on Union Street in Boston.
Jessica Rinaldi/Boston Globe With World Cup tourism and the temporary easing of restrictions related to public drinking and last call, Boston’s nightlife is on the upswing so far this summer.

title: Motorcyclist falls 65 feet to riverbank after crashing into guardrail in N.H.
author: John Waller
bullets: Local News The motorcyclist suffered "catastrophic injuries," officials said.

By John Waller June 24, 2026 | 11:38 AM 1 minute to read A motorcyclist ended up severely injured and several hundred feet away from his bike early Monday morning after a crash in Manchester, New Hampshire.
When firefighters arrived at the crash site near Granite Street on the Interstate 293 north off-ramp at about 1:30 a.

title: 2 seriously injured when motorcycle hits deer in N.H.
author: John Waller
bullets: Local News The crash occurred Monday afternoon.
By John Waller June 24, 2026 | 11:02 AM Less than a minute to read ShareSend this article to your social connections.
Two Quebec residents were seriously injured Monday afternoon when their motorcycle struck a deer in northern New Hampshire, throwing them from the bike.

title: Leave it to England to rain on Boston’s World Cup parade
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: World Cup "VAR went for a coffee.
" Thomas Tuchel's frustration sums up England's day in a 0-0 World Cup draw with Ghana at "Boston Stadium" (Gillette Stadium).
Via Hussein Hammouda/Boston.

title: Justin Long and Kate Bosworth dined at this popular Somerville brunch spot
author: John Waller
bullets: Celebs "They liked the cream of wheat!
" By John Waller June 24, 2026 | 10:33 AM 1 minute to read Apparently Justin Long and Kate Bosworth enjoy dining under grape leaves.
The Hollywood couple dined at the Neighborhood Restaurant in Somerville on Tuesday, the brunch eatery — known in part for its grapevine-shaded patio — shared on its social pages.

title: Bemused Beckham, Scottish-bashing Mumfords spotted cheering on England in Foxborough
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Celebs Alexandra Daddario and Kit Harington were also spotted among the World Cup crowd.

So apparently Marcus Mumford wasn’t just making conversation when he pumped up his native England (and dissed our new favorite people in the world, the Scottish) at Monday’s Mumford & Sons show at Fenway Park.
The band wasted no time hoofing it out to Boston (née Gillette) Stadium the next day to root on their countrymen in person.

title: Brad Stevens was noncommittal on Jaylen Brown’s Celtics future, and other takeaways from NBA Draft presser
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Boston Celtics "We have a great relationship and an open relationship where we talk about everything, but I don't want to predict the future.

" Brad Stevens (David L Ryan/Globe Staff The pivot that the Celtics are about to make following their failed pursuit of Giannis Antetokounmpo remains one of the biggest national storylines of the offseason.
So, it was no surprise that many of the questions directed at Stevens during his press availability following round one of Tuesday’s NBA Draft were centered around Jaylen Brown.
